perturbation.original_text: Upon identification of a Project, the Parties shall jointly prepare a proposed plan for the acquisition and execution of the Project, which shall include a detailed forecasted budget therefore. Upon the award of a prime contract regarding a Project to Lloyds, the Parties shall attempt in good faith to negotiate and enter into a subcontract agreement whereby the scope of Lightbridge's services on the Project, and compensation to Lightbridge therefore, shall be specified.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon identification of a Project, the Parties shall jointly prepare a proposed strategy for the acquisition and execution of the Project, which shall include a high-level budget outline. Upon the award of a prime contract regarding a Project to Lloyds, the Parties may decide to negotiate a sub-agreement, and compensation to Lightbridge therefore, shall be generally discussed.
perturbation.explanation: The terms 'plan' is replaced with 'strategy', 'detailed forecasted budget' with 'high-level budget outline', 'shall attempt in good faith to negotiate and enter into a subcontract agreement' with 'may decide to negotiate a sub-agreement', and 'shall be specified' with 'shall be generally discussed'. This perturbation creates uncertainty because 'strategy' implies a broader, less defined approach compared to 'plan,' and a 'high-level budget outline' offers less financial clarity than a 'detailed forecasted budget.' Furthermore, changing 'shall attempt' to 'may decide' makes the subcontract negotiation optional, creating uncertainty around Lightbridge's involvement and compensation. Finally, switching 'shall be specified' to 'shall be generally discussed' undermines contractual obligations. The contradiction occurs on whether Lightbridge's services will be mandatory after winning a project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lloyds shall act as prime contractor on any Project and that Lightbridge shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lloyds, and shall be entitled to perform such work which would generate up to twenty-five percent (25%) of the total fees payable to Lloyds as general contractor under the Project.
perturbation.justification: The changed text replaces 'shall attempt in good faith to negotiate and enter into a subcontract agreement' with 'may decide to negotiate a sub-agreement,' and 'shall be specified' with 'shall be generally discussed'. This directly contradicts the original intent in the contradicted text, which states that Lightbridge 'shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lloyds, and shall be entitled to perform such work'. The change introduces uncertainty regarding whether Lightbridge's services will be mandatory, conflicting with the original requirement of Lightbridge having a right of first refusal and entitlement to perform work. This falls under the 'Misaligned Terminology - In Text Contradiction' because the terms related to the agreement of Lightbridge's services are changed, leading to conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall present to the other Party every potential Project which such Party desires to pursue. Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Project, regardless of which Party identified such Project, the Parties agree that Lloyds shall act as prime contractor on any Project and that Lightbridge shall have a right of first refusal to participate in such Project as a sub- contractor to Lloyds, and shall be entitled to perform such work which would generate up to twenty-five percent (25%) of the total fees payable to Lloyds as general contractor under the Project.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party should consider presenting to the other Party potential ventures which such Party might explore. Section 2.2 Relationship of Parties. With respect to any Venture, regardless of which Party proposed such Venture, the Parties hope that Lloyds would act as prime operator on any Venture and that Lightbridge could be considered to participate in such Venture as a possible sub-contributor to Lloyds, and might be qualified to perform certain tasks that could correspond to an estimated portion of the total considerations possibly remitted to Lloyds as primary operator under the Venture.
perturbation.explanation: Here, the terms 'Project' has been swapped with 'Venture'. Additionally, the mandatory and binding language has been changed, where 'shall' is replaced with more wishful thinking words such as 'should consider', 'might explore', 'hope', 'could be considered', and 'might be qualified'. This creates contradictory conditions on the relationship between each party regarding the Projects/Ventures, and makes it uncler on which conditions need to be satisfied.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Each of the following events constitutes an event of default (an "Event of Default") by a Party (the "Defaulting Party") under this Agreement: 5.1.1 the Defaulting Party fails to perform in any material respect any obligation under this Agreement. or any representation or warranty made by the Defaulting Party in this Agreement is untrue in any material respect, and such failure is not cured within 30 days after the non-Defaulting Party notifies the Defaulting Party of such failure; or
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that each party 'shall' present potential projects, establishing a clear obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to 'should consider presenting,' introducing ambiguity. Furthermore, the original text stated that Lloyds 'shall act' as the prime contractor, whereas the modified version only expresses a 'hope' that Lloyds 'would act' as the prime operator. These changes directly contradict the Event of Default clause (5.1.1), which states that a 'failure to perform in any material respect any obligation under this Agreement' constitutes a default. By weakening the mandatory language regarding the presentation of projects and Lloyds' role as prime contractor, the modified text makes it unclear whether a failure to present projects or have Lloyds act as prime operator would constitute a default, thus creating a contradiction and unenforceability between the modified section and section 5.1.1.

perturbation.original_text: Each Party shall, and shall cause its Affiliates to. at all times, comply in all respects with all applicable Laws in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement. Without limiting the generality of the foregoing, each Party (the "Relevant Party") shall not, and shall cause its Affiliates not to, do or cause to be done any of the following in connection with the transactions contemplated by this Agreement: (a) directly or indirectly through any other Person. pay, offer to pay, promise to pay, or authorize the payment of any monies or anything of value to any official or employee of any Government Authority or any political party or candidate for political office for the purpose of illegally or improperly inducing or rewarding any action by such official, employee or candidate favorable to the Relevant Party or any of its Affiliate or (b) take any other action that, if taken by a Person subject to United States law, would violate the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that is familiar with the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act.
perturbation.changed_text: Each Party will strive to, and will attempt to cause its Affiliates to, from time to time, consider adhering to various principles related to applicable regulatory guidelines in connection with the initiatives described in this Understanding. Without diminishing the breadth of the preceding statement, each Party (the "Participating Entity") might not, and will suggest its Affiliates not to, engage in or facilitate any of the subsequent actions concerning the arrangements outlined in this Understanding: (a) explicitly or implicitly, via any intermediary, remit, propose, commit, or sanction the disbursement of any funds or tangible assets to any representative or personnel of any Public Institution or any political organization or aspirant for political candidacy with the aim of inappropriately influencing or incentivizing any conduct by said representative, personnel, or candidate beneficial to the Participating Entity or its Associated Entities; or (b) undertake any measure that, if executed by an entity governed by United States statutes, could potentially contravene the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act. Each Party recognizes and accepts that they may have some level of awareness regarding the United States Foreign Corrupt Practices Act.
perturbation.explanation: Multiple terms have been made unclear or weak: Laws -> regulatory guidelines; transactions -> initiatives; comply in all respects -> strive to... consider adhering to; shall not -> might not; Relevant Party -> Participating Entity; any Government Authority -> Public Institution; political party -> political organization; Affiliate -> Associated Entities; for the purpose of illegally or improperly inducing or rewarding any action -> with the aim of inappropriately influencing or incentivizing any conduct. Each Party acknowledges and agrees that is familiar with -> Each Party recognizes and accepts that they may have some level of awareness regarding. This change makes the language less committal and clear, thus making the legality and requirements less clear. The section on compliance is now weakened to suggestions and acknowledgments, creating uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The terms of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England.
perturbation.justification: The original text stipulated that each party "shall comply in all respects with all applicable Laws," thereby creating a firm obligation. The changed text weakens this obligation to "strive to... consider adhering to various principles related to applicable regulatory guidelines." This introduces a significant contradiction, because the contradicted text states that "The terms of this Agreement shall be governed by and construed in accordance with the laws of England." The weakened language in the changed text creates ambiguity about whether the parties are actually bound by the laws of England, thus contradicting the governing law clause.
